ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Dec-16-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard together: PTR-12-296344 The Marjorie Brandon Trust & PCN-12-296346 Conservatorship of Marjorie Alice Brandon.
Appearances: Lizabeth DeVries for and with Debra Dolch.

Ms. DeVries advised the Court that Ms. Brandon passed away this morning and that they will be making arrangements for her disposition. The Court asked counsel to discuss the fees requested on the conservatorship and trust. Ms. DeVries stated that Ms. Brandon had a tenuous
medical and financial situation, that she had properties held in a variety of ways, that they are still trying to dissolve the corporation, that there were title issues on various properties including Indian Tribe issues, that family members were an issue and objected to everything at every step of the way, that after Betty Foote died the fees became less as they no longer had to deal with all of the issues brought on by Ms. Foote and that they do not expect a lot of fees for the next phase. The Court asked about the fees requested by the conservator. Ms. DeVries replied that there were a lot of services to provide for, that the family created agitation to the conservatee to the extent that one of the Doctors contacted the Court Investigator to request that the family members be restrained. Ms. Dolch reported that there were many psychiatric hospitalizations, that at some points 5 caregivers were needed at a time, that a concierge doctor would come 3-5 times per week, that the family wanted a more holistic approach and Ms. Brandon to live with Ms. Foote and it was tried and did not work out, that Ms. Brandon had an active social life, that she had a car and driver and would go on outings with her two caregivers and that they had a really nice party for her on Friday which she enjoyed. The Court asked about the $4500.00 hotel bill. Ms. Dolch stated that there was a significant bed bug issue that required intensive fumigation, that Ms. Brandon and her two caregivers stayed at the Fairmont while this was happening, confirmed that there was $80,000.00 in medical expenses not covered by her insurance and confirmed that the estate paid for caregiver meals and that it is not uncommon. The Court took the accounting petitions in both cases under submission.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Chan. (Not Reported)

Off Calendar Feb-28-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lizabeth De Vries for and with Debra Dolch (Petitioner). Charles Jonas Court appointed Counsel for Marjorie Brandon (Settlor/Trustee/Conservatee) in the Conservatorhsip matter. Betty Foote (Sister of Marjorie) in Propria Persona. Lea Holtorf (Sister of Marjorie) also present.

Mr. Jonas requested that he be appointed to represent Marjorie Brandon in this trust matter. Ms. De Vries stated that she agrees with Mr. Jonas' request. The Court granted said request. Mr. Jonas is to be prepare the order. Mr. Jonas stated that he reviewed the pleadings, that the relief sought by Ms. Rasch is appropriate, that the Court should grant the petition and appoint Ms. Dolch as Successor Trustee, and that Bank of The West declined to act as Co-Trustee. Ms. Foote presented an endorsed copy of a declaration she filed yesterday, and stated that she agrees with Debra Dolch going ahead with managing her Sister's assets and the Trust continuing under Court supervision. Ms. Foote also expressed concern with regard to not being able to visit with her Sister, her Sister being a target for elder abuse, asked that her Sister be referred to as "temporarily incapacitated" and stated that she would give her apartment up so that her Sister can return home. Ms. De Vries stated that the challenges between the Family is not before the Court, that the only issue before the Court is appointment of Debra Dolch and that there is a proper procedure to deal with the issue of placement and capacity when those issues arise. Ms. Foote also expressed to the Court that the two places Marjorie calls home are San Francisco and Calistoga, stressed the importance of maintaining them to allow Marjorie her freedom of choice and stated that she wanted to be informed. Mr. Jonas stated that his Client expressed that she would be distressed if any family member would be involved. The Court reminded the Parties of the importance to keep Marjorie's best interest in mind. The Court granted the Petition for Removal of Marjorie Brandon as Trustee Due to Incapacity, Appointment of Debra Dolch as Sole Successor Trustee, Continued Court Supervision, etc. (subject to review of the order). Bond in the amount of $300,000.00 is required. All Powers of Attorney are no longer in effect.
